differentials.raw: Serosa  echogenic / hyperechoic Most benign conditions preserve the gut signature involving long segments of bowel, while malignant

context: A Ring =  contracted / hypertrophied muscles in response to incompetent GE sphincter Location:   at tubulovestibular junction = superior aspect of vestibule √  usually 2 cm proximal to GE junction at upper end of vestibule √  varies in caliber during the same examination, may disappear on maximum distension √  broad smooth narrowing with thick rounded margins √  visible only if tubular esophagus above + vestibule below are distended B Ring =  sling fibers representing a U-shaped thickening of inner muscle layers with open arm of U toward lesser curvature = inferior aspect of vestibule Location
